heading: Teacher AI Tools
search_queries: AI tools classroom teacher productivity high school
AI-assisted lesson plans high school English History EdTech
search_strategy: The MagicSchool/Copilot/NotebookLM/Grammarly/Brisk rotation is over-covered. Do not re-cover unless concrete new feature, new partnership, or district-level news. Prefer: peer-reviewed pedagogy research on AI use outcomes (learning gains, not surveys), district implementation stories past 7 days, new tools debuting at ISTE/FETC 2026, state policy/regulation, student voice/equity pieces, AI-grading controversies. OECD 2026 Digital Education Outlook and nerdbot.com April 14 roundup are now covered.

covered.one_line_summary: OECD 2026 report on AI integration in K-12 and higher-ed globally; includes equity-gap findings.

covered.title: nerdbot.com April 14 AI teacher tools roundup
covered.one_line_summary: April 14 2026 roundup of emerging AI tools for classroom teachers.

ongoing_narrative: The teacher-AI-tools thread through mid-April 2026 has added two new primary sources: the OECD 2026 Digital Education Outlook (a policy-level anchor with cross-national equity data, adding institutional weight to the adoption stats) and the nerdbot.com April 14 roundup (a fresh tool-discovery source that sits outside the over-cited EdWeek/eSchoolNews rotation). The structural story is unchanged: MagicSchool (5M+ educators), Microsoft Copilot Teach, Google NotebookLM, AFT $23M National Academy — the big anchors — are rotation-complete. The live debate remains efficiency vs instructional use (EdTech Mag, eSchoolNews), and the equity lens (OECD, The 74 Million) is the most under-reported angle. Next: peer-reviewed learning-gains research (not surveys), district implementation past 7 days, ISTE/FETC 2026 debuting tools, state AI-classroom policy, student voice pieces, AI-grading controversies.
